definition: First-mover advantage in AI adoption creates 18-month wealth window before commoditization; competitors lacking urgency fall behind.
rationale: Centroid-backed; all members address the time-bound window and adoption speed advantage. Rename clarifies the dual insight: window timing + competitive urgency.

definition: Extract stories and moments from actual business activity (calendars, calls, events) and package as authentic proof via AI repurposing.
rationale: Kick 'Information vs Curation'—epistemological claim about value of curation vs free info; orthogonal to tactical content extraction. Core 6 members cohere on business-activity-sourcing.

definition: New opportunities appear perfect because their hidden costs remain invisible; focus fixing what you have rather than chasing new.
rationale: Tight psychological frame—all members reinforce observer bias toward competitors' success. Centroid-backed; high min_intra_sim.

kick_members: Lead Generation Channels and Content Strategy
definition: Demonstrate expertise and past work via aligned incentives, transparency, and education to overcome cold-start trust gap.
rationale: Kick 'Fee Psychology' (artificial fees increasing commitment is behavioral mechanism, not trust-building tactic). Kick 'Lead Generation Channels' (multi-channel distribution orthogonal to trust). Core 7 members cohere on transparency + proof tactics.

definition: Negotiate lower vendor costs, resell at market rate, pocket the spread; core economic play across services, staffing, and multiples.
rationale: All 6 members are spread-capture variations (ad cost, staffing cost, practice valuation multiples). Tight economic thesis; centroid-backed.

definition: Identify and prune low-ROI volume; consolidate to high-margin offers; target audience that loves you over broad appeal.
rationale: All 7 members address profit density (calendar efficiency, CAC ceiling, channel focus, event ROI, audience selectivity, overhead cuts). Centroid-backed.

definition: Distinctiveness requires continuous active work; entropy naturally pulls toward normalcy. Rarity preserves pleasure; profit requires resistance.
rationale: All 4 members cohere on psychological/thermodynamic resistance to staying differentiated. Centroid-backed; tight mechanism.

definition: Convert raw attention through free content → email list → paid advisory via multiple price-point products; balance giving and asking.
rationale: All 8 members address the funnel from free reach to monetized conversion. Centroid-backed; high min_intra_sim.

canonical_name: Centers of Influence as True Customer
definition: Reposition intermediaries (CPAs, financial advisors, agencies) as the primary customer; they refer rather than execute.
rationale: All 4 members cohere on flipping customer identification. 'Cobblers shoes problem' stays because it explains *why* agencies miss this shift.

definition: Ground strategy in mathematical/physical constraints, not convention; mathematically superior models replace (not augment) predecessors.
rationale: Small (2 members) but tight thesis. Both ground decision-making in physics, not norms. High min_intra_sim.

definition: Structure cold-to-close via VSL + pre-frame + qualification + close; frameworks like C-L-O-S-E-R move close rates from 6% to 20%+.
rationale: Kick 'Critical thinking for closing deals'—tautological (A9: critical thinking is essential for closing is trivially true). Core 9 members cohere on systematic sales structure.

definition: Avoid the middle: anchor high (5-10x tiers), bundle aggressively, price for best customer not everyone; high anchor elevates entire perception.
rationale: All 12 members address price psychology (anchoring, bundling, premium tiers, luxury positioning). Centroid-backed; tight mechanism across variations.

definition: Long-term thinking (1-5-10 year horizon) is last remaining alpha; most founders optimize short-term and lose to patient competitors.
rationale: All 5 members cohere on time horizon as differentiator. Centroid-backed; Chick-fil-A / Ray Dalio examples anchor the frame.

definition: Founder/CEO personal outreach to top talent outperforms recruiters; pair with strong brand + systems to attract without juniors.
rationale: All 5 members cohere on founder-led, high-touch recruiting. Centroid-backed; network advantage clear.

definition: Scale output per person via offshore low-cost execution + documented systems; add leadership roles only when workload forces it.
rationale: All 6 members cohere on lean-first, offshore-second, systems-third scaling model. Centroid-backed; clear from owner-operator to multi-role.
